text: True or False: Snowflake provides a way to restore a table that was accidentally dropped using the UNDROP command.
Which of the following describes a 'Transient' table?

options.a: It exists only for the duration of a single session.
options.b: It has no Fail-safe period but persists across sessions.
options.c: It is a read-only copy of a Permanent table.
options.d: It is stored in the Cloud Services layer instead of the Storage layer.

text: When unloading data, what does the 'MAX_FILE_SIZE' parameter control?

options.a: The maximum size of the total data set to be unloaded.
options.b: The maximum upper limit of an individual file size created on the stage.
options.c: The maximum number of rows per file.
options.d: The compression ratio of the files.

text: True or False: A Virtual Warehouse can be configured to automatically scale based on the number of queries in the queue.
Which command is used to see the list of files currently residing in an Internal Stage?

options.c: SELECT * FROM @stage_name
options.d: DESC STAGE @stage_name

text: Which role is primarily responsible for creating and managing users and roles within the RBAC hierarchy?

options.c: PUBLIC
options.d: ACCOUNTADMIN

text: What is the primary benefit of 'Search Optimization Service' in Snowflake?

options.a: It speeds up large table scans.
options.b: It improves the performance of point lookup queries on large tables.
options.c: It reduces the cost of data storage.
options.d: It automates the creation of clustering keys.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the use of standard SQL for querying semi-structured data like XML and JSON.
Which of the following are characteristics of a 'Temporary' table? (Choose two.)

options.a: It persists after the session ends.
options.b: It is only visible to the user who created it.
options.c: It does not incur any storage costs.
options.d: It is dropped automatically at the end of the session.

text: What is the maximum Time Travel retention period for a Transient table?

options.c: 7 days
options.d: 90 days

text: Which function allows you to look into the contents of a VARIANT column and produce a set of rows (one for each element)?

options.c: SPLIT()
options.d: EXPLODE()

text: True or False: Data in Snowflake is encrypted using a hierarchical key model managed by Snowflake.
How does Snowflake charge for the Cloud Services layer?

options.b: It is charged only if usage exceeds 10% of daily compute credits.
options.c: It is a flat monthly fee.
options.d: It is charged per user per hour.

text: What does 'Clustering Depth' indicate for a table?

options.a: The number of columns in the clustering key.
options.b: How well the table is partitioned relative to the clustering key.
options.c: The total size of the micro-partitions.
options.d: The time it takes to scan the table.

text: True or False: You can share a secure view that joins data from multiple databases within your account.
Which tool is the primary CLI (Command Line Interface) for connecting to Snowflake?

options.c: SnowTerm
options.d: SnowShell

text: What happens to the 'Load History' of a table if the table is truncated?

options.c: The load history is reset for that day only.
options.d: The table cannot be truncated if it has load history.

text: True or False: Multi-cluster warehouses are available in the Standard Edition of Snowflake.
Which view provides information about the credits consumed by 'Auto-Clustering'?

options.c: WAREHOUSE_METERING_HISTORY
options.d: TABLE_STORAGE_METRICS

text: What is the primary difference between a 'Standard' and an 'Enterprise' edition account regarding Time Travel?

options.b: Standard is limited to 1 day; Enterprise allows up to 90 days.
options.c: Standard allows up to 90 days; Enterprise allows up to 365 days.
options.d: There is no difference.

text: Which statement is true regarding Snowflake's support for ACID properties?

options.a: Snowflake only supports Atomic and Consistent properties.
options.c: Snowflake is ACID compliant only for single-row inserts.
options.d: Snowflake does not support ACID properties.

text: True or False: To use the 'PUT' command, you must use a Snowflake client like SnowSQL or a driver.
Which column in the Query History page helps identify the Warehouse that ran the query?

options.c: Compute Resource
options.d: Cluster Name

text: What is the impact of a 'Full Outer Join' on two very large tables that are not clustered similarly?

options.a: High probability of query failing due to timeout.
options.b: Increased probability of spilling data to remote storage.
options.c: The query will automatically trigger auto-clustering.
options.d: No impact, Snowflake handles this via Cloud Services.

text: True or False: When sharing data, the Data Provider pays for the storage and the Data Consumer pays for the compute used to query the shared data.
Which Snowflake object allows you to group multiple SQL statements together and execute them as a single unit?

options.c: UDF
options.d: Sequence

text: In a Multi-cluster Warehouse, what does the 'Economy' scaling policy prioritize?

options.b: Conserving credits by keeping clusters busy.
options.c: Automatically resizing clusters.
options.d: Enabling MFA for all users.

text: True or False: Snowflake allows the creation of 'External Tables' that query data sitting in an S3 bucket without importing it.
Which of the following describes 'Fail-safe' storage? (Choose two.)

options.a: It is configurable by the user.
options.b: It provides 7 days of additional protection after Time Travel.
options.c: It can be used to recover data from any point in the last 90 days.
options.d: It can only be accessed by Snowflake Support.

text: What is the primary reason for a 'Virtual Warehouse' to be in a 'Starting' state for a long period?

options.a: Wait time for cloud provider to provision resources.
options.b: The warehouse is too small for the query.
options.c: MFA is required to start the warehouse.
options.d: The database is currently being cloned.
